title: Single-Pixel Imaging (random basis compressive sensing)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD contains a widefield-fluorescence template rather than single-pixel-specific content (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in the canonical mine_example/spc.md reference and standard compressed-sensing literature.

E.description: A single photodetector integrates scene radiance weighted by a sequence of m modulating patterns (Bernoulli, Gaussian, or structured bases); each pattern yields one scalar measurement. The m scalars form the compressed measurement vector.
E.forward_model: y = Phi * vec(x) + n,  Phi in R^{m x n}, m << n
E.world_state_x: 2D (or 3D) scene x in R^n where n = H*W (vectorized), non-negative radiance
E.observation_y: m scalar photodetector readings y in R^m (additive Gaussian read-noise; Poisson under low-light)
physical_parameters_theta: Phi: sensing matrix (rows are illumination patterns), normalized
m: number of measurements (determines sampling ratio m/n)
pattern basis: Bernoulli {+1,-1}, Gaussian, Hadamard, noiselet, etc.
detector gain, dark offset, read noise sigma

W.regime: Underdetermined (m << n) compressive recovery; random sensing matrices with i.i.d. Bernoulli/Gaussian entries satisfy RIP w.h.p. when m >= C * s * log(n/s) for s-sparse signals in a known basis.

C.solver_class: L1-proximal (FISTA, ISTA, SPGL1), TV (GAP-TV), learned (LISTA, ISTA-Net)
C.error_bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma / sqrt(m) + C2 * ||x - x_s||_1 / sqrt(s) (Candes-Romberg-Tao)
C.complexity: O(m * n) per iteration (dense Phi); O(n * log n) for structured Phi (Hadamard, noiselet)
